name: koppeltaal

metadata.name: koppeltaal
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
Koppeltaal Python connector
===========================

**Warning:** This code is for the so-called Koppeltaal-1 specification. As of Dec 31 2024 all Koppeltaal-1 technical infrastructure is decomissioned and this code base is obsolete. It is left here for historical purposes.

“Koppeltaal” (Ducth for "Connect language") is a technical solution based on
the international HL7/FHIR standard. It enables the exchange of e-health
interventions. Koppeltaal enables organizations to connect e-health
interventions from other providers to their own IT environment. With
Koppeltaal organizations can more easily mix and match the best of the
available e-health interventions and applications.

See https://koppeltaal.nl/

This connector acts as an intermediary or adapter between application and framework code and a Koppeltaal server. It is written in the Python programming language.

See https://python.org

This Koppeltaal connector was initially developed by Minddistrict Development B.V. for Stichting Koppeltaal.

Setting up for development
---------------------------

Previously `buildout` was used for setting up the package for development. We now rely on using a *virtual env*, *pip* and a requirements file instead.

Quick start:

```sh
# inside the Koppeltaal-Python-Connector checkout
$ python3.8 -m venv .
$ ./bin/pip install -r requirements -e .
```

Or if you use [pipenv](https://github.com/pypa/pipenv):
```sh
# inside the Koppeltaal-Python-Connector checkout
$ pipenv install -r requirements -e .
```

Tests
-----

We use the [pytest] framework. The tests should be run against the Koppeltaal `edge` server, preferrably in a domain sepcifically setup for running the
tests.

```sh
$ bin/py.test --server=edge
```

The `--server=edge` argument to the test command is the server to connect to when running tests. It is taken from `~/.koppeltaal.cfg`. The format of
`~/.koppeltaal.cfg` looks like this:

```
[edge]
url = https://edgekoppeltaal.vhscloud.nl
username = PA@PythonAdapterTesting4Edge
password = <secret here>
domain = PythonAdapterTesting
```

The name of the configuration section in the `~/.koppeltaal.cfg` file is the name passed to the `--server` argument.

Note how there're two webdriver/selenium tests. They require a Firefox "driver" to be available on your system. For MacOS using brew, this can be installed like so:

```sh
$ brew install geckodriver
```

*Tox* is used for running the test suites for multiple Python versions including 2.7, 3.6, 3.7 and 3.8. Python 2 compatibility is supported throug [six].

Command line interface
----------------------

To use the koppeltaal connector command line interface:

```sh
$ bin/koppeltaal --help
```

Arguments:

The first argument to the *koppeltaal* script is the server to connect to, for
example *edge*. The username, password and domain can be passed in as arguments or taken from `~/.koppeltaal.cfg`.

Metadata / Conformance statement
--------------------------------

To retrieve the Conformance statement from the server:

```sh
$ bin/koppeltaal [servername] metadata
```

Activity definition
-------------------

To get the activity definition from the server:

```sh
$ bin/koppeltaal [servername] activities
```

Messages
--------

To get a list of messages in the mailbox:

```sh
$ bin/koppeltaal [servername] messages
```

You can filter on a patient (with *--patient*), or event (with
*--event*) or status (with *--status*):

```sh
$ bin/koppeltaal [servername] messages --status=New --event=CreateOrUpdateCarePlan
```

To get a specific message:

```sh
$ bin/koppeltaal [servername] message [message URL or id]
```

Python API
----------

Use the following API in your integration code to talk to a Koppeltaal server:

T.B.D.

[buildout]: http://www.buildout.org
[pytest]: https://pytest.org
[six]: http://six.readthedocs.io/

methods.name: koppeltaal.testing.MockTransport.expect
documentation:
Register an URL that the transport expects to be called for.

Fixture(s) is the contents that the transport returns when the
corresponding expected URL is called. When registering fixtures for
the same URL multiple times, they are returned in that order.

Note how the data that is to be sent to the expected URL is stored in
the `called` attribute when indeed the expected URL is called.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.KoppeltaalError
documentation: Generic koppeltaal error.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.TransportError
documentation: There was an error related to the transport.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.ConnectionError
documentation: There was a connection error from the transport.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.ResponseError
documentation: The transport got a response but it was an error.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.OperationOutcomeError
documentation: A non 200 status with an operation outcome was returned by the server.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.MessageResponseError
documentation: The response in the message header reported an error.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.DummyError
documentation: Error generated when you ask the dummy connector to perform an action.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.InvalidBundle
documentation: A malformed bundle was obtain from the koppeltaal server.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.InvalidValue
documentation:
A value does not match the definition for its corresponding field
in the koppeltaal definitions.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.InvalidReference
documentation: A value that is not a valid reference.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.InvalidCode
documentation: A code appear that is not expected in its definition.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.InvalidSystem
documentation: The system mentioned by the code is not allowed/supported here.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.InvalidResource
documentation: A resource type mismatch expected in the koppeltaal definitions.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.RequiredMissing
documentation:
A field is required in the koppeltaal definitions but its value is
missing.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IFHIRResource
documentation: A resource that can be sent to the koppeltaal server.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IBrokenFHIRResource
documentation: A resource that was broken.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IReferredFHIRResource
documentation:
A resource that is referred but missing from the resource payload
or bundle.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IIdentifiedFHIRResource
documentation: A resource that can be identified with a self link.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IIntegration

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IIntegration.transaction_hook
documentation:
Optional hook to integrate sending back a message into a
transaction system.

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IIntegration.model_id
documentation:
Return a (unique) id for the given `model`. Should stay the same id
if called again with the same model.

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IIntegration.link
documentation: Return fhir URL for `model` which is a `resource_type`.
Personalizing of connector settings and behavior.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IUpdate

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IUpdate.success
documentation: Mark the message as succeed.

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IUpdate.fail
documentation: Mark the message as failed, with the given exception message.

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IUpdate.postpone
documentation:
Do nothing. The message will stay in claim an eventually revert
back to new.

Update retrieved from a message via the connector. It can be used a
context manager to automatically update the processing status of
the message.

classes.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IConnector

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IConnector.metadata
documentation: Return the conformance statement.

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IConnector.activities
documentation: Return a list of activity definitions.

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IConnector.activity
documentation:
Return a specific activity definition identified by `identifier` or
None.

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IConnector.launch
documentation: Retrieve launch URL for an activity in a careplan for a give user..

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IConnector.launch_from_parameters
documentation:
Retrieve launch URL out of all required parameters. Those
parameters can be extracted from a careplan.

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IConnector.updates
documentation:
Iterate over the available new messages in the mailbox for
processing.

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IConnector.search
documentation: Return a list of messages matching the given criteria.

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IConnector.send
documentation: Send an update about event with data for patient.

methods.name: koppeltaal.interfaces.IConnector.close
documentation: Close any connection left to the server.
Connector to interact with the koppeltaal server.
